class_schedule.topic: Capstone Project Kickoff and Client Briefing
learning_objectives: Scope a real-world UX project from a client brief
Define project goals, constraints, and success criteria
Create a project plan with research, design, and testing phases
readings: Just Enough Research by Erika Hall, Ch. 1

lecture_notes.introduction: The capstone project is your opportunity to apply everything you have learned to a real-world problem for a real client or organization. This is not a class exercise — it is professional-grade UX work.
key_concepts: Client brief analysis: identifying stated and unstated needs
Project scoping: what is achievable in 15 weeks
Success criteria definition: how the client will evaluate your work
Phase planning: discovery (3 weeks), definition (2 weeks), design (4 weeks), testing (3 weeks), delivery (3 weeks)
Weekly check-ins and milestone reviews
lecture_notes.demonstration: Presenting the capstone client briefs and walking through the scoping process for each — defining what is in scope, what is out, and what the deliverables will be

review.value: The biggest capstone risk is scope creep. Define a clear, achievable scope upfront and manage it rigorously. A well-executed narrow scope beats a poorly executed broad scope every time.
Define upfront how the project will be evaluated — measurable usability improvements, stakeholder satisfaction, design system completeness. Without criteria, you cannot demonstrate success.

class_schedule.topic: Discovery Phase: Research Planning and Execution
learning_objectives: Plan and execute user research for the capstone project
Synthesize research findings into actionable design direction
Present research findings to the client with clear recommendations
readings: Interviewing Users by Steve Portigal, Ch. 5-8
Observing the User Experience by Goodman, Kuniavsky & Moed, Ch. 5

lecture_notes.introduction: Discovery research is the foundation of your capstone. The quality of your research determines the quality of everything that follows — wrong assumptions here cascade into wrong designs.
key_concepts: Research method selection based on project constraints and questions
Recruiting real users: leveraging client relationships for participant access
Competitive analysis in the capstone context
Research synthesis: from raw data to design principles and opportunity areas
Client research readout: presenting findings with recommendations
lecture_notes.demonstration: Research synthesis workshop: using real early-stage capstone research data to practice affinity diagramming, insight extraction, and design principle formulation
setup_requirements: Research tools appropriate to chosen methods

review.value: Present findings, not just data. Clients need: 'We found that 4 of 6 users could not find the pricing page, which costs an estimated $X in lost leads. We recommend restructuring the navigation with pricing in the top-level menu.'

Distill research into 3-5 design principles that will guide all design decisions: 'Transparency builds trust,' 'Progress over perfection,' 'One task at a time.' These keep the team aligned throughout design.

class_schedule.topic: Design Phase: Ideation and Concept Development
learning_objectives: Generate multiple design concepts grounded in research findings
Evaluate concepts against research-derived design principles
Present design concepts to the client for direction setting
readings: Creative Confidence by Tom Kelley, Ch. 5-6
Lean UX by Jeff Gothelf, Ch. 5-6

lecture_notes.introduction: The design phase translates research insights into tangible solutions. Generating multiple concepts before committing to one ensures you are solving the right problem the right way.
key_concepts: Concept generation: multiple directions explored before converging
Concept evaluation: scoring against design principles and user needs
Client concept review: presenting options with trade-off analysis
Design decision documentation: recording rationale for future reference
Iterative refinement: evolving the chosen concept through multiple rounds
lecture_notes.demonstration: Capstone design review: students present 2-3 concept directions for their projects, receive feedback from instructor and peers, and select the direction for development

review.value: Presenting a single concept feels like a take-it-or-leave-it proposition. Presenting 2-3 concepts with trade-off analysis gives the client informed choice and demonstrates thorough thinking.

Record why each design decision was made — what research supported it, what alternatives were considered, what trade-offs were accepted. This documentation is invaluable for your portfolio case study.

learning_objectives: Plan and conduct usability testing for the capstone design
Prioritize findings and iterate on the design based on test results
Document the iteration process for the final deliverables
readings: Rocket Surgery Made Easy by Steve Krug, Ch. 6-10
Measuring the User Experience by Tullis & Albert, Ch. 10

lecture_notes.introduction: Testing is where your design meets reality. Every capstone must include real user testing that validates your design decisions and drives meaningful iteration.
key_concepts: Test planning: recruiting from the client's user base
Testing the right things: focusing on critical flows and risky assumptions
Rapid iteration: fix-retest cycles within the capstone timeline
Measuring improvement: before/after metrics where baseline exists
Documenting iteration: showing how testing changed the design
lecture_notes.demonstration: Capstone testing review: students present their test plans, get feedback on task scenarios and methodology, and discuss how to handle limited time for iteration

review.value: Prioritize testing the design decisions you are least confident about — the novel interaction pattern, the reorganized navigation, the new terminology. Validating what you already know works is less valuable.

Document every change driven by testing with before/after comparisons. This is the most compelling content for your portfolio case study — it proves your design improved through evidence, not opinion.

class_schedule.topic: Delivery Phase: Final Presentation and Handoff
learning_objectives: Prepare professional deliverables for client handoff
Present the capstone project to the client and evaluation panel
Reflect on the project process and document learnings
readings: Presenting Design Work by Donna Spencer, Ch. 7-9
Articulating Design Decisions by Tom Greever, Ch. 8-10

lecture_notes.introduction: The final delivery is your chance to demonstrate the full arc of professional UX practice — from research through design through validated outcomes. The presentation must tell the story of why your solution works.
key_concepts: Client deliverable package: final designs, design system, specifications, research summary
Developer handoff documentation: annotations, interactions, responsive behavior
The capstone presentation: 20 minutes telling the story of problem to solution to impact
Post-project reflection: what worked, what you would do differently, what you learned
Portfolio case study extraction: turning the capstone into your strongest portfolio piece
lecture_notes.demonstration: Final presentation rehearsal: students present their capstone stories in the full 20-minute format with Q&A, receiving final feedback before the client presentation

review.value: Context and problem (3 min) > Research findings and insights (4 min) > Design concepts and rationale (5 min) > Testing and iteration with evidence (4 min) > Final solution and impact (3 min) > Next steps (1 min)

Professional handoff means a developer or designer can continue your work without asking you questions. Annotated designs, interaction specifications, design tokens, and a clear README of design decisions.

major_assignments.title: UX Design Capstone Project
major_assignments.description: Execute a complete, real-world UX design project for a client or organization. Deliver research, design, testing, and a final presentation demonstrating the full UX design process with measurable outcomes.
objectives: Scope and manage a professional UX project through all phases
Conduct real user research and translate findings into design
Create high-fidelity designs tested and iterated with real users
Deliver professional-grade client deliverables and presentation
deliverables: Research report with findings, insights, and design principles
High-fidelity designs (20+ screens) with design system components
Interactive prototype of the primary user flow
Usability test report with iteration documentation (before/after)
Client presentation (20 minutes) with all project deliverables
Portfolio case study documenting the entire project

criteria: Research methods appropriate to the project questions
Sufficient participants for meaningful findings
Insights are actionable and directly inform design decisions
Research is well-documented and professionally presented
Designs solve the identified user problems
Visual design is polished and consistent
All screen states addressed
Design system components are reusable and well-documented
Testing conducted with real users from the target audience
Findings are specific and prioritized
Iteration evidence shows measurable improvement
Testing methodology is rigorous
Client deliverables are professional and complete
Presentation tells a compelling story
Handoff documentation enables continuation without you
Q&A responses demonstrate deep project understanding
Milestones met on schedule
Client communication was professional and timely
Scope was managed effectively
Process was well-documented throughout
Rigorous research leading to genuine insights
High-quality designs that solve real user problems
Evidence-based iteration through user testing
Professional deliverables and compelling presentation

capstone_project.description: The complete capstone project evaluated across all phases: research, design, testing, and delivery

milestone_reviews.description: Quality and timeliness of milestone deliverables throughout the project

client_presentation.description: Final client presentation quality and Q&A handling

portfolio_case_study.description: Capstone translated into a compelling portfolio case study

peer_feedback.description: Quality of feedback provided to classmates during milestone reviews

course_policies.attendance: All milestone review sessions are mandatory. Missing a client presentation is a failing grade for that component. Weekly check-ins are expected.
course_policies.late_submissions: Milestones have firm deadlines tied to client expectations. Late milestone deliverables lose 20% per day and risk client relationship.
course_policies.collaboration: Capstone projects are individual. Peer feedback is encouraged and graded. Sharing deliverables between students is prohibited.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All work must be your own. The client relationship is real — misrepresenting your capabilities or deliverables is both academic dishonesty and professional malpractice.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Figma, research tools as appropriate, usability testing equipment, presentation software, and project management tool.
